description:
You are a mobile build engineer doing routine maintenance on the CI data that the iOS and Android pipelines produce.

Current data location  
• A single file already exists at  
  /home/user/projects/mobile-ci/pipeline_runs.csv  

The CSV file is semicolon-separated and has exactly four fields in this fixed order:

        run_id;platform;branch;result

Example (one physical line in the file):
        20231015-001;android;main;SUCCESS

Required end-state  
1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/projects/mobile-ci/reports  
   if it is not present.

2. Inside that directory create exactly TWO text files, with names and **precise content/format** as follows.

   a) /home/user/projects/mobile-ci/reports/android_main_pass.txt  
      • Contains only the run_id values (nothing else) of the rows in pipeline_runs.csv  
        where platform == "android", branch == "main" and result == "SUCCESS".  
      • Sort the selected run_id values in ascending (lexicographic) order before writing.  
      • One run_id per line.  
      • Every line, including the last line, MUST end with a single UNIX newline character “\n”.  
      • No extra blank lines or trailing spaces are allowed.

   b) /home/user/projects/mobile-ci/reports/ios_dev_fail.txt  
      • Contains only the run_id values (nothing else) of the rows in pipeline_runs.csv  
        where platform == "ios", branch == "develop" and result == "FAILURE".  
      • Sort the selected run_id values in ascending (lexicographic) order before writing.  
      • One run_id per line.  
      • Every line, including the last line, MUST end with a single UNIX newline character “\n”.  
      • No extra blank lines or trailing spaces are allowed.

Hints  
• Grep/awk/cut plus sort are sufficient; expect to need only 2-3 shell commands in total.  
• Remember to create the reports directory before trying to write into it.  
• Pay close attention to newlines and ordering—the automated test compares these files byte-for-byte.

Success criteria  
The task is considered complete when both files exist at the exact paths given above and their contents match the specifications perfectly.
truth:
Pre-existing file (visible to the agent)

Path: /home/user/projects/mobile-ci/pipeline_runs.csv  
Exact contents (10 physical lines, each ending with “\n”):

20231015-001;android;main;SUCCESS
20231015-002;android;main;FAILURE
20231015-003;ios;main;SUCCESS
20231015-004;ios;develop;FAILURE
20231015-005;ios;develop;SUCCESS
20231015-006;android;develop;SUCCESS
20231015-007;ios;develop;FAILURE
20231015-008;android;main;SUCCESS
20231015-009;android;main;SUCCESS
20231015-010;ios;develop;FAILURE

Expected files after the agent finishes

1. /home/user/projects/mobile-ci/reports/android_main_pass.txt  
   Contents (each line terminated with “\n”):

20231015-001
20231015-008
20231015-009

2. /home/user/projects/mobile-ci/reports/ios_dev_fail.txt  
   Contents (each line terminated with “\n”):

20231015-004
20231015-007
20231015-010

The automated tests will:
• Verify that /home/user/projects/mobile-ci/reports exists and is a directory.  
• Compare android_main_pass.txt and ios_dev_fail.txt byte-for-byte against the expected contents above.  
• Ensure no additional files are created inside the reports directory.
